summary: setka-editor 999.9.9 exfiltrates host identifiers and CI secret presence to hardcoded IP on install
details: Package publishes at version 999.9.9 — the maximum-resolvable semver — under a name that targets internal/private namespaces, a classic dependency-confusion vector. package.json declares both `preinstall` and `postinstall` hooks that run `node callback.js`. callback.js collects the installer's OS user, uid, hostname, cwd, local network interfaces, external IP (via https://api.ipify.org), CI environment metadata, and the presence/absence of installer-side secrets (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, DOCKER_PASSWORD), then POSTs the collected JSON to a hardcoded plain-HTTP endpoint at http://132.243.20.244:8000/api/collect with User-Agent `dependency-confusion/setka-editor`. The package self-describes as a 'Dependency Confusion Hunter' PoC, but the on-install behavior is operationally identical to a live dependency-confusion attack against any organization that has an internal package named setka-editor: `npm install` resolves 999.9.9 from the public registry and ships installer reconnaissance data to an attacker-controlled IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
